title: hieracium haematopodum subsp. coloratipes
intro: Hieracium haematopodum subsp. coloratipes, also known as Red Hawk Hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium haematopodum plant, belonging to the dandelion family and native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Red Hawk Hawkweed include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb for treating infections and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to treat skin irritations and wounds, or internally to support digestive health.
